DECLARATION OF MICHAEL MCGINTY SUPPORTING HIS MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND SET ASIDE VOID JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 59749858) FILED BY DEFENDANT MCGINTY, MICHAEL
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-28-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND SET ASIDE VOID JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. "WHEN A DEFENDANT CHALLENGES THE COURT'S PERSONAL JURISDICTION ON THE GROUND OF IMPROPER SERVICE OF PROCESS THE BURDEN IS ON THE PLAINTIFF TO PROVETHE FACTS REQUISITE TO AN EFFECTIVE SERVICE." (SUMMERS V. MCCLANAHAN (2006) 140 CAL.APP.4TH 403, 413, INTERNAL QUOTES OMITTED.) DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO GRANT THE MOTION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473(D). DEFENDANT DECLARES THAT THE APARTMENT WHERE THE PROCESS SERVER LEFT THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT HAS BEEN HIS RESIDENCE SINCE JANUARY 2004, THERE HAS NEVER BEEN A FEMALE NAMED JULIA WHO RESIDED THERE SINCE JANUARY 2004, AND HE DOES NOT KNOW ANYONE MATCHING THE DESCRIPTION OF "JULIA" WHO COULD HAVE ANSWERED HIS DOOR WHEN THE PROCESS SERVER CLAIMS TO HAVE LEFT THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT. (MCGINTY DECLARATION, 3-6.) PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING IT PROPERLY SERVED DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF INCORRECTLY RELIES ON CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473.5 AND CIVIL CODE 1788.61 TO ARGUE THAT THIS MOTION IS UNTIMELY. (SEE PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION, 3:12-5:3.) DEFENDANT BRINGS THE MOTION PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 473, SUBD. (D) ON THE BASIS THAT THIS COURT LACKS PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT DUE TO LACK OF PROPER SERVICE. (SEE DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION, 1:21-27.) ACCORDINGLY, THERE IS NO TIME LIMIT FOR BRINGING THIS MOTION. THE REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/JQ

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Michael Mcgintys Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Set Aside Void Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JACOB HARKER, TEL. NO. 1-415-624-7602 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Michael McGinty's motion to quash service of summons and set aside void judgment is GRANTED. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process the burden is on the plaintiff to prove ' the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413, internal quotes omitted.) Defendant has shown good cause to grant the motion under Code of Civil Procedure 473(d). Defendant declares that the apartment where the process server left the summons and complaint has been his residence since January 2004, there has never been a female named Julia who resided there since January 2004, and he does not know anyone matching the description of "Julia" who could have answered his door when the process server claims to have left the summons and complaint. (McGinty Declaration, 3-6.) Plaintiff has failed to satisfy its burden of showing it properly served Defendant. Plaintiff incorrectly relies on Code of Civil Procedure 473.5 and Civil Code 1788.61 to argue that this motion is untimely. (See Plaintiff's Opposition, 3:12-5:3.) Defendant brings the motion pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 473, subd. (d) on the basis that this Court lacks personal jurisdiction over Defendant due to lack of proper service. (See Defendant's Notice of Motion, 1:21-27.) Accordingly, there is no time limit for bringing this motion. The requests for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/jq
